title: Rattle Apply Operations (Configurator + BOM + Document tiers)
description: Idempotent ensure_* operations executed by rattle-config-builder. 15 operation types across 3 tiers (configurator: 7, BOM: 3, documents: 5). Each operation is name-keyed → get-or-create-or-PATCH; a second run is a safe no-op. See agents/rattle-config-builder.md § 'Document- and BOM-tier operations' and skills/rattle-apply-config/references/operations-contract.md for the canonical narrative.

recommendation_source.description: Path or hash identifying where the payload came from (recommendation, variant-bom, techdoc-template, or hand-edited).

decimal_string.description: Backend stores prices as Numeric(10,2) and serialises them as decimal strings on the wire (e.g. "500.00"). Accept either a numeric value or the canonical 2-decimal string; the builder coerces.

parent_product.description: Convenience: link the area to a single product on creation. Maps to AreaCreateRequest.product_id (looked up by name). For multi-product areas, omit and use a separate POST /products/{id}/areas call.

link_to_areas.description: Names of every area across all products that this group should be linked to. The builder resolves names to ids and posts the full id array in ONE call (POST /groups/{id}/areas with body {area_ids: [int, ...]}) — not one call per area.

price_scalings.description: JSON dict keyed by stringified option id, mapping to a scaling descriptor. Same three shapes as option_scalings: legacy bare numeric, ratio {opt, part}, range {areas: [{min, max, part}]}. See skills/rattle-bom-builder/references/option-scalings.md.

option_description.description: Per-area override of Option.description. Backend field is option_description (NOT 'description' — AreaConfigUpdateRequest has extra=forbid).

ensure_constraint_pair.description: ATOMIC REPLACE semantics: POST /constraints with body {product_id, forbidden: [...]} replaces ALL pairs for the product in one call. The builder accumulates all ensure_constraint_pair operations for the same product and emits a single replace request with read-modify-write + X-Constraints-Version. Server returns 409 Conflict (NOT 412) on stale version.

area.description: Optional area scope (matches ForbiddenRule.area_id; null for product-wide rules).

rule_json.description: Conditional-rule shape evaluated by app/utils/constraint_solver._rule_active and app/models.py ForbiddenRule.violates. Shape: {requires: [<clause>...], invalid: [<option_id>...]}. NOTE: the {if, then} shape some legacy OpenAPI examples show is REJECTED by the runtime evaluator — the rule will save but never fire.

requires.description: Array of clauses. The rule fires only when EVERY clause is satisfied; clauses are AND-folded by the solver.

invalid.description: Set of option ids that become forbidden when 'requires' is satisfied. Operations may carry option NAMES — the builder resolves them to ids before POSTing (see operations-contract.md § 7).

rule_clause.description: Rule clause as evaluated by app/utils/constraint_solver._clause_true. Use exactly one of anyOf / allOf / groupSelections per clause; default operator is OR. Option / group references may be ids (post-resolution) or names (the builder resolves names → ids before POSTing).

anyOf.description: Clause is satisfied when ANY of these option ids is selected.

allOf.description: Clause is satisfied when ALL of these option ids are selected.

groupSelections.description: Map of stringified group_id → list of allowed option ids; clause is satisfied when at least one option per listed group is selected.

usage_subclauses.description: Conditional-inclusion DSL evaluated by app/utils/conditions.evaluate_subclauses. See skills/rattle-bom-builder/references/usage-subclauses.md for the full grammar.

option_scalings.description: Quantity-scaling dict keyed by stringified option id. Three descriptor shapes: legacy bare numeric, ratio {opt, part}, range {areas: [{min, max, part}]}. See skills/rattle-bom-builder/references/option-scalings.md.

ensure_part.description: Natural key (company_id, part_number). Maps to PartCreateRequest (extra=forbid). part_cost is INTEGER (not decimal). Ghost parts (bom_structure='ghost') are forced to part_cost=0 server-side.

ensure_part_placement.description: Natural key (part_id, area_id). Maps to PartPlacementCreateRequest (extra=forbid). quantity > 0 enforced server-side.

area.description: Area name; the builder resolves to area_id.

ensure_bom_item.description: Natural key (parent_part_id, child_part_id, alt_group, effective_from, effective_to). Maps to BomItemCreateRequest (extra=forbid). part_group_id is read-only on the wire — do NOT include it. quantity > 0 enforced. NOTE: at the time of writing, app/routes/api_v1/parts.py:633-647 silently drops effective_from / effective_to on POST and PATCH — agents relying on date-scoped variants should verify post-write that the dates landed.

ensure_template.description: Natural key (company_id, name). Maps to DocumentTemplateCreateRequest. doc_type values: offer, quote, technical_doc, ccms, custom (legacy plurals offers/quotes also accepted; the legacy alias technical_documentation is rejected on writes).

ensure_structure_block.description: Natural key (template_id, slug) — the StructureBlock UniqueConstraint. parent_id is NOT in the unique constraint; siblings under different parents that share a slug collide → 409.

parent_slug.description: Slug of parent structure block (null for root chapters).

ensure_attachment.description: Natural key (structure_block_id, content_block_id). Maps to AttachmentCreateRequest (extra=forbid). conditions is a list of dicts (NOT condition_json — wrong field name returns 422).

content_block_key.description: Resolved to content_block_id by the builder.

ensure_content_block.description: Natural key (company_id, key). Shared blocks have product_id=null. is_dynamic is COMPUTED (membership in DYNAMIC_BLOCK_KEYS) — do NOT send is_dynamic=true on writes; use the canonical key (e.g. 'dynamic:document_configuration') instead.

ensure_block_locale.description: Locale upsert. Two flavours: structure_block_locale (URL-encoded language segment, body just {title}) and content_block_locale (body {language, version?, blocks?, template_name?, is_active?} — 'blocks' is the WIRE field for the block_json column; 'blocks' and 'template_name' are mutually exclusive on create). The structure-block locale URL upper-cases the language.

template_name.description: For scope='structure': the template the structure block belongs to.

title.description: For scope='structure': the only payload field.

blocks.description: For scope='content': EditorJS array (mutually exclusive with template_name). Wire field is 'blocks'; storage column is 'block_json'.

template_name_dynamic_slot.description: For scope='content': dynamic slot name like 'dynamic:document_configuration' (mutually exclusive with blocks). Sent as 'template_name' on the wire — renamed here only to avoid collision with the structure-block 'template_name' key.
